title: Add score-page Associated Datasets panel with label distribution
description:
## Summary
Add a score-page "Associated Datasets" panel with label distribution visibility.

## Purpose
Give operators immediate insight into dataset inventory, size, and class balance for a score.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Panel lists datasets associated by `scoreId` in newest-first order.
- Each dataset shows item count and label-distribution visualization.
- Legacy datasets with missing stats show a clear "stats unavailable" state.
- UI behavior and labels use associated-dataset terminology.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting score-page Associated Datasets panel implementation. Plan: fetch DataSet records by scoreId, resolve dataset_stats from datasource-version YAML metadata, and render rows + distribution bars + legacy no-stats state.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented score-page Associated Datasets panel in dashboard/components/ui/score-component.tsx. Behavior: fetches datasets by scoreId (newest-first), resolves persisted dataset_stats from datasource-version YAML metadata, shows per-dataset row count + label distribution segmented bar/legend, and renders explicit legacy state when stats are unavailable. Loading/error/empty states included. Validation notes: frontend lint attempt failed due existing local AJV/eslintrc runtime issue (`missingRefs` / `defaultMeta`) unrelated to this change path.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Completed in branch; awaiting manual UI review in running dashboard session.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Post-implementation UI refinements applied per operator feedback: (1) removed border-heavy styling in Associated Datasets area/rows in favor of flat bg-card/bg-background regions, (2) panel now collapsed by default and shows only latest dataset until expanded, (3) dataset ID now uses standard IdentifierDisplay component, (4) removed per-dataset title line, (5) rows count + class distribution now aligned on bottom row (left/right) under distribution bar, and (6) score component container now supports vertical scrolling to avoid pushing main configuration content off-screen.
